name: MaintenEase
description: Modern maintenance management software (CMMS). Flat monthly pricing instead of per-user seats.

solutions.name: Work Order Software
solutions.tagline: Capture, assign, and close work orders without the spreadsheet sprawl.

solutions.name: Preventive Maintenance Software
solutions.tagline: Schedule by date, runtime, or meter — and watch reactive work shrink.

solutions.name: Facility Maintenance Software
solutions.tagline: One system for assets, work orders, PMs, and tenant requests across every building.

solutions.name: Fleet Maintenance Software
solutions.tagline: Meter-based PMs, DVIRs, and compliance reports — without the spreadsheet.

solutions.name: Maintenance Request Portal
solutions.tagline: Standard requests and an urgent lane — submissions land straight in your inbox.

solutions.name: Asset Tracking Software
solutions.tagline: QR codes, locations, custody, and full service history — for every asset you own.

solutions.name: Asset Management Software
solutions.tagline: Register, schedule, maintain, and report on every asset — in one place.

glossary.short: Software that centralizes work orders, assets, and preventive maintenance schedules so teams stop relying on spreadsheets and paper.
Scheduled service performed before equipment fails — by calendar date, runtime hours, or meter reading — to extend asset life and avoid downtime.
A formal request that describes a maintenance task — what needs doing, on which asset, by whom, when — and tracks it from creation to completion.
All the work required to keep a building and its systems safe, comfortable, and operational — HVAC, plumbing, electrical, lighting, life safety, and the building envelope.
Keeping a group of vehicles — trucks, vans, cars, heavy equipment — safe, compliant, and on the road through scheduled service and quick reactive repairs.
The supplies, parts, and consumables a maintenance team uses to keep operations running — separate from raw materials that become finished products.
The day-to-day and scheduled work needed to keep a building's structure, systems, and finishes in safe and serviceable condition.
Ongoing upkeep of residential or commercial rental property — units, common areas, grounds, and life-safety systems — to protect the asset and keep tenants happy.
Fixing equipment after it has already broken down — the unplanned, run-to-failure work that a maintenance program tries to shrink.
Using live condition data — vibration, temperature, current — to predict failures and service equipment just before it would break, not on a fixed calendar.
Planned repair work that fixes a known defect found during an inspection or PM — before it becomes a breakdown.
Servicing equipment when a monitored condition (temperature, vibration, pressure) crosses a defined threshold — not on a fixed schedule.
The average operating time between one failure and the next for a repairable asset — a core reliability metric maintenance teams track to spot bad actors.
The average time it takes to restore a failed asset to working order — a key maintainability metric that captures how fast your team responds.
